data_in.description: MS/MS spectra with annotated MS1 precursor masses and ground-truth subformula labels for held-out test set

data_out.description: Per-peak subformula predictions as a structured table (peak m/z, predicted subformula, rank, confidence score)
Evaluation metrics table including per-peak accuracy, exact-match rate per spectrum, and top-k ranking statistics

domain_knowledge: The MIST-CF subformula assignment component operates without SIRIUS fragmentation trees, instead using a learned neural network that assigns subformulas to peaks based on precursor formula constraints and observed m/z differences.

Subformula predictions must satisfy mass-balance constraints: the sum of fragment subformula masses must account for observed peak m/z and ionization mode (positive mode with multiple adduct types including [M+H]+).
Ranking metrics for subformula assignment evaluate whether the ground-truth label appears in the top-k predictions and penalize incorrect or missing chemical formulas at each peak position.
The formula transformer architecture learns data-dependent patterns rather than relying on fixed fragmentation rules, allowing prediction across diverse molecule classes without database queries.

direct_checks: verify file exists in repository samgoldman97/mist-cf containing subformula assignment implementation (e.g., Python module or configuration file naming or documenting internal subformula protocol)
verify that subformula assignment component does NOT invoke SIRIUS fragmentation tree computation (scan codebase for absence of SIRIUS API calls or tree construction in subformula assignment pathway)
verify that held-out test set exists in repository or linked deposit (Zenodo/GitHub) with annotated MS/MS spectra and reference per-peak subformula labels
file_format_is: output predictions file (CSV, JSON, or TSV) with at least columns: [peak_m_z, predicted_subformula, reference_subformula] or equivalent
row_count_equals or file_exists: number of predictions in output must match number of peaks in held-out test set (verify row count or file completeness)
script_runs: end-to-end inference script on held-out spectra completes without error and produces per-peak subformula predictions
expert_review: chemical validity: predicted subformulas for each MS/MS peak are chemically plausible (e.g., no impossible charge states, mass balance consistent with precursor formula and fragmentation logic)
assignment accuracy: per-peak subformula predictions match or closely align with reference annotations on held-out set (requires domain judgment of acceptable deviation or match criteria)
independence from SIRIUS: verification that subformula assignment uses learned embeddings or energy-based scoring (per SCARF/transformer approach) rather than pre-computed fragmentation trees

Apply MIST-CF internal subformula assignment (formula transformer neural network) to rank candidate subformulas for each fragment peak without invoking SIRIUS or external databases.

task_description: Implement the internal subformula assignment component of MIST-CF that assigns chemical subformulas to MS/MS fragment peaks without invoking SIRIUS fragmentation trees. Evaluate the per-peak subformula label predictions on a held-out test set of spectra and report accuracy metrics.

task_inputs.label: MS/MS spectra with MS1 precursor masses and ground-truth subformula labels

task_objective: Implement and validate the internal subformula assignment mechanism that assigns chemical formulas to individual MS/MS peaks without SIRIUS fragmentation trees.

task_outputs.label: Per-peak subformula predictions with ranks and confidence scores
Accuracy, exact-match rate, and ranking statistics

title: Reconstruct the internal chemical subformula assignment protocol replacing SIRIUS fragmentation trees
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load MS/MS spectra and corresponding MS1 precursor masses from the held-out test dataset. 2. Apply the MIST-CF internal subformula assignment protocol (neural network-based formula transformer) to rank candidate subformulas for each observed fragment peak, conditioned on the precursor formula and observed m/z value. 3. For each spectrum, generate per-peak subformula predictions without computing SIRIUS fragmentation trees or querying external spectrum databases. 4. Compare predicted subformula assignments against reference labels and compute per-peak accuracy, exact-match rate per spectrum, and ranking metrics (e.g., fraction of correct subformulas in top-k predictions). 5. Report evaluation metrics and landmark errors on the held-out set.
